name: Single-Leg Hip Thrust
resistance_profile: Tension peaks at full lockout with the glute completely contracted; load falls away as the hips drop toward the floor.
cues: Upper back on a bench, one foot planted
Drive the hip up, squeeze the glute
execution_steps: Rest your upper back against a bench and plant one foot flat on the floor.
Lift the other leg off the floor, keeping the knee bent or extended.
Brace your core and keep your hips level, ribs down.
Drive through the planted heel to lift your hips to full lockout.
Squeeze the working glute at the top, then lower under control.
good_when: You want to add load to bodyweight glute training without a barbell.
You need to fix side-to-side strength imbalances in the glutes.
You train at home and want a harder progression than the two-leg bridge.
bad_when: You are a beginner still learning the basic bridge (start two-legged).
You want to move maximal absolute load (choose the barbell hip thrust).
